component: IP allow list address rules per feature allow list in Splunk Cloud Platform deployment in Google Cloud regions
info: This is the hard limit per IP feature allow list. For example, the IP allow list service limit for collecting data is separate from sending search queries. Customers specify the IP address or IP address range that is permitted to access Splunk Cloud Platform and those from which Splunk Cloud Platform can collect data (forwarders and HEC) and send search queries. These are generically referred to as IP allow list rules. These rules can be configured to use CIDR blocks to maximize the IP allow list coverage.

component: IP allow list address rules per allow list group in Splunk Cloud Platform deployment in AWS regions
info: This is the hard limit per IP allow list group. For example, the service limit for collecting data is separate from sending search queries. Customers specify the IP address or IP address range that is permitted to access Splunk Cloud Platform and those from which Splunk Cloud Platform can collect data (forwarders and HEC) and send search queries. These are generically referred to as IP allow list rules. These rules can be configured to use CIDR blocks to maximize the IP allow list coverage.

component: IP allow list address rules per allow list group in Splunk Cloud Platform deployment in Azure regions
info: This is the hard limit per IP allow list group. For example, the service limit for collecting data is separate from sending search queries. Customers specify the IP address or IP address range that is permitted to access Splunk Cloud Platform and those from which Splunk Cloud Platform can collect data (forwarders and HEC) and send search queries. These are generically referred to as IP allow list rules. These rules can be configured to use CIDR blocks to maximize the IP allow list coverage.
Larger CSV lookups will not replicate across a Search Head Cluster and will be quarantined on the Search Head it was generated. We recommend using KV store for lookups larger than 1 GB.

Dynamic Data Active Archive enables restoring data up to 10% of your Active Searchable storage. If you no longer need already restored data, you can clear it before it expires to reclaim space to perform additional restores.

Dynamic Data Active Archive restorations were not designed to exceed 25 TB. If more data than 25 TB is needed, split your desired time frame into multiple smaller restores less than the recommended size limit.
This is the maximum total number of Edge Processor instances. As a reminder, an Edge Processor can be made up of 1 or multiple Edge Processor instances.

component: Number of applied pipelines per Edge Processor
info: This is the maximum total number of applied pipelines per Edge Processor. Pipelines with longer SPL2 configurations may reduce this maximum limit.

component: Total traffic through an Edge Processor network
info: This is the maximum total amount of traffic running through all deployed instances of Edge Processors for each Splunk Cloud Platform environment.
This is a hard limit of the Splunk Cloud Platform email relay service. Use an email distribution list to increase the number of email recipients.
This is a hard limit of the Splunk Cloud Platform email relay service.

component: HEC maximum content length size limit
info: There is a recommended limit to the HEC payload size in Splunk Cloud Platform to ensure data balance and ingestion fidelity. A HEC request can have one or more Splunk events batched into it but the payload size should be no larger than this limit. If you exceed this limit, you may experience performance issues related to data balance and ingestion fidelity.
This is the maximum size of a single collection that is tested with KV Store per Splunk Cloud Platform environment.
This is the total maximum recommended size of KV Store across all collections per Splunk Cloud Platform environment.

This is the maximum tested limit for the self-service Splunkbase and private app management. If you exceed this soft service limit, you may experience issues with performing self-service app management.

component: Federated search for Amazon S3 and Federated Analytics
info: This is the maximum configurable implicit default limit for federated search for Amazon S3. You can change this limit by reaching out to Splunk Support.

component: Federated search for Amazon S3 and Federated Analytics
info: This is the maximum configurable overall data volume limit for federated search for Amazon S3. You cannot use a LIMIT clause to create searches that exceed that volume limit. You can change this limit by reaching out to Splunk Support..

This is the maximum tested limit for the number of Splunk Cloud Platform and Splunk Enterprise remote deployments used with federated search for Splunk. If you exceed this soft service limit, you may experience issues with performing federated search for Splunk.
Check if still needed.
Check if still needed.
This is the maximum tested limit for the number of active indexes per Splunk Cloud Platform environment. If you exceed this soft service limit, you may experience issues.

component: Maximum number of pipelines per tenant
limitation: 50 pipelines for either Essentials tier or Premier tier
info: Maximum number of pipelines per tenant for Ingest Processor. For more information on Ingest Processor subscription tiers, see Subscriptions in the Use Ingest Processors manual.

limitation: 3 days Essentials tier / 14 days Premier tier
info: Ingest Processor supports persistent queues (PQ) alongside in-memory queues to prevent data loss during system congestion. For more information, see Persistent queues in the Use Ingest Processors manual. For more information on Ingest Processor subscription tiers, see Subscriptions in the Use Ingest Processors" manual.
Check if still needed.

This is the hard limit of the maximum Knowledge Bundle replication size. If the Knowledge Bundle exceeds this service limit, the search tier will not push the bundle to the indexer tier. Searches on the indexer tier will instead use the previously pushed bundle, which will be within the size limit.

component: Maximum search concurrency per Splunk Cloud Platform environment.
limitation: 400 for entitlement of more than 900 SVC or 7 TB
info: This is the standard limit to the number of ad hoc and scheduled searches that Splunk Cloud Platform environment can concurrently admit as tracked in metrics.log. Search concurrency limits apply to searches initiated either from the Cloud search tier or from on-premises hybrid and federated search heads.  If you require search concurrency beyond the standard limit, you may be able to do so through optimizing your existing search workload or by contacting your Splunk sales representative to increase your SVC entitlement. For more information on setting percentages of concurrency for scheduled and summarization searches, see Configure Search Settings in Splunk Cloud Platform.

component: Maximum ES search concurrency per Splunk Cloud Platform environment
limitation: 150 for Victoria experience / 78 for Classic experience
info: When you add an Enterprise Security subscription to Splunk Cloud Platform, additional search processes are available for it that are in addition to the search concurrency included in the Splunk Cloud Platform subscription. This is the standard limit to the number of searches that Enterprise Security can concurrently admit as tracked in metrics.log. If you require ES search concurrency beyond the standard limit, you may be able to do so through optimizing your existing search workload or by contacting your Splunk sales representative to increase your SVC entitlement.

limitation: 600 for Victoria experience / 600 for Classic experience
info: This was the limit tested for Enterprise Security on Splunk Cloud Platform. Note that there are different service limits for the Victoria and Classic experiences. A correlation search is a type of scheduled search. Correlation searches are a part of Enterprise Security, and are used to generate notable events or execute other adaptive response actions. If your use case exceeds the tested limit and is deemed to be causing performance issues, the remediation is to change the configured limit to no more than the tested limit. See Correlation search overview for Splunk Enterprise Security.

limitation: 840,000 searches/day for entitlement of more than 166 SVC or 1 TB for Victoria Experience
info: This is the maximum tested limit of Enterprise Security specific searches that can be executed successfully in a well-tuned system.  If you exceed this soft service limit, you may experience issues with scheduled search completion. Note that other factors such as search concurrency limit or the nature of searches may additionally limit the number of successful searches that run.

limitation: 95 for Victoria experience / 75 for Classic experience
info: This was the limit tested for Enterprise Security on Splunk Cloud Platform. Note that there are different service limits for the Victoria and Classic experiences. Data models and data model acceleration are critical components of Enterprise Security. To provide the best experience possible for customers, we suggest a maximum of 9 accelerated models. The most common data models deployed are: Change, Endpoint, Authentication, Intrusion Detection, Network Sessions, Network Resolution, Network Traffic, Web, and Performance. If your use case exceeds the tested limit and is deemed to be causing performance issues, the remediation is to change the configured limit to no more than the tested limit. See Configure data models for Splunk Enterprise Security.

component: Event Analytics / Notable Event Aggregation Policies
info: You can configure up to 100 Notable Event Aggregation Policies with 50/50 division between time-based and non-time-based NEAPs.
You can ingest up to 100,000 alerts per minute into Event Analytics with your Correlation Searches.

When you add an IT Service Intelligence subscription to Splunk Cloud Platform, additional search processes are available for it. This starting point scales up at higher ingestion rates and also for workload-based subscriptions.
You can configure up to 100 Correlation Searches.

limitation: 1000 Services per Service Template (having 5 KPIs and 40 Entities)
info: You can configure up to 1000 Services per Service Template if the Service Template is configured with the 5 KPIs and 40 Entities.

limitation: 300 Services per Service Template (having 10 KPIs and 150 Entities)
info: You can configure up to 300 Services per Service Template if the Service Template is configured with the 10 KPIs and 150 Entities.

limitation: 600 Services per Service Template (having 10 KPIs and 75 Entities)
info: You can configure up to 600 Services per Service Template if the Service Template is configured with the 10 KPIs and 75 Entities.

The join command combines the results of a subsearch with the results of a main search. This limit is the maximum number of result rows in the output of a subsearch that can be joined against a main search. For more information, see the join command in the Splunk Cloud Platform Search Reference.

limitation: 700,000 searches/day for entitlement of less than 166 SVC
info: This is the maximum tested limit of scheduled searches that can be scheduled successfully. Note the subscription tiers and applicable service limit.  If you exceed this soft service limit, you may experience issues with scheduled search completion. Note that other factors such as search concurrency limit or the nature of searches may additionally limit the number of successful scheduled searches that run.

limitation: 1.5 M searches/day entitlement of more than 166 SVC
info: This is the maximum tested limit of scheduled searches that can be scheduled successfully. Note the subscription tiers and applicable service limit.  If you exceed this soft service limit, you may experience issues with scheduled search completion. Note that other factors such as search concurrency limit or the nature of searches may additionally limit the number of successful scheduled searches that run.

info: When you add these Premium Apps subscriptions to Splunk Cloud Platform, additional search processes are available for each Premium App. These search processes are exclusive to the Premium Solution subscription.
